prompt: List every AI startup that raised a round of $300M+ OR crossed a $5B valuation in 2026 (January-present, today is June 2026). For each: company name, round size, valuation, date, one-line description, category. Be exhaustive and specific. Cite sources (TechCrunch, Crunchbase, Bloomberg, The Information, X posts).

AI infrastructure, inference, and compute startups that raised $50M+ in 2025-2026. For each: name, amount, date, valuation if known, one-line, and the SPECIFIC wedge/differentiator. Include inference providers, GPU clouds, serving, orchestration. Cite sources.

AI agent and agentic-workflow startups that raised $50M+ in 2025-2026. For each: name, amount, date, the wedge, and any traction/ARR. Cover horizontal agent platforms, agent infra, and notable vertical agents. Cite sources.
AI developer-tools and coding startups that raised $50M+ in 2025-2026 (coding agents, IDEs, code review, testing, CI). For each: name, amount, date, wedge, traction. Cite sources.

Vertical and applied AI startups (legal, healthcare, finance, sales, support, marketing, security, insurance, construction, etc.) that raised $50M+ in 2025-2026. For each: name, vertical, amount, date, wedge. Cite sources.
AI data, evaluation, observability, RAG, and memory startups that raised $50M+ in 2025-2026. For each: name, amount, date, what they do, the wedge. Cite sources.

Which AI startups have the fastest ARR growth on record in 2025-2026 (e.g., fastest to $1M, $10M, $100M ARR)? Name the companies, their ARR trajectory with timeframes, and what they sell. Cite sources.

Analyze the biggest and fastest-growing AI startups of 2025-2026. What do the winners have in common? Which categories, business models, pricing models, and wedges are attracting the most capital AND growing fastest? Synthesize the pattern with specific company examples and a clear thesis. Cite sources.

Using X and news from the LAST 60 DAYS only, list the newest notable AI startup funding rounds, launches, and breakouts. For each: company, what it does, amount/stage, date, why notable. Quote specific X posts. Cite sources.
List AI companies that became new unicorns ($1B+) or decacorns in 2026. For each: name, valuation, what they do, why they got there fast. Cite sources.
Which AI products and companies are growing fastest by revenue and usage in 2026? Include ARR milestones, user/usage growth numbers, and what's driving the growth. Cite sources.

Using X right now, what AI tools, products, or companies are builders, founders, and engineers most excited about and talking about most in the last 30 days? What is getting real traction (not hype)? Quote specific recent X posts with handles. Cite sources.

What new AI tools and products launched in 2026 are seeing rapid adoption among developers and startups? Cite Product Hunt, GitHub trending, Hacker News, and X. For each: what it does and evidence of traction.
Which AI product categories are seeing the fastest adoption and spend growth in 2026, and which categories are cooling or saturating? Give evidence. Cite sources.
What AI open-source projects and repos are trending or growing fastest in 2026 (stars, adoption)? For each: what it does and why it's growing. Cite sources.

What major models, features, and products have OpenAI, Anthropic, Google/DeepMind, xAI, Meta, DeepSeek, Mistral, and Alibaba/Qwen shipped in 2026 (last 6 months)? List with dates and what each enables. Cite sources.

Which AI startup categories or specific products have been commoditized, disrupted, or killed by frontier-model releases and native platform/lab features in 2025-2026? Give concrete examples of startups that got disrupted or had to pivot. Cite sources.

What capabilities are frontier labs and platforms (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Cursor, hyperscalers) now building NATIVELY that previously required third-party startups (memory, eval, agents, computer use, browsing, code execution, RAG, connectors, fine-tuning)? Cite sources.

How are AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Cloudflare, Databricks, and Snowflake encroaching on the AI tooling and infrastructure startup space in 2026 (gateways, eval, fine-tuning, agents, governance, vector/RAG)? Which startups are threatened? Cite sources.

What do AI experts, VCs, and lab leaders predict will be commoditized or absorbed by frontier models and platforms in the next 6-18 months? What is 'in the path of the bulldozer'? Quote specific predictions from X and articles. Cite sources.

State of open-weight models in mid-2026 (NVIDIA Nemotron 3, Qwen3.5, DeepSeek V4, gpt-oss, GLM, Llama, Mistral). Parity with closed models, licensing terms, and which businesses/categories this commoditizes. Cite sources.

What kinds of AI businesses are structurally PROTECTED from being commoditized by frontier labs and hyperscalers in 2026 (proprietary data moats, distribution, regulation, deep workflow lock-in, real-world/physical integration, trust/liability)? Give specific examples and the reasoning for each. Cite sources.

Using X, what are AI startup founders and engineers complaining about or struggling with most when building AI products in 2026? Quote specific recent posts with handles and dates. Group into themes. Cite sources.
What AI tools and infrastructure are startups and small AI teams actually PAYING for in 2026 (not free or open-source)? Where is real, proven willingness-to-pay? Cite sources.
What are the biggest unsolved pain points in building, deploying, debugging, and operating AI agents in production in 2026? Quote engineers on X and engineering blogs. Cite sources.
What do indie hackers and solo founders building AI micro-SaaS want and need in 2026? What playbooks are getting them to revenue fastest? Quote specific examples and revenue numbers. Cite sources.

What AI security, governance, and compliance pains are enterprises and startups facing in 2026 (shadow AI, data leakage, EU AI Act, agent permissions/identity, prompt injection)? What are they actually buying and budgeting for? Cite sources.

Using X and forums, what AI products or tools do founders and engineers EXPLICITLY say they wish existed in 2026 ('someone should build...', 'why is there no...')? Quote specific posts verbatim with handles. Cite sources.
In 2026, which buyer personas and departments have fresh budget to spend on AI tools, and what are they buying? Where are new budget lines appearing? Cite sources.

What are top VCs (a16z, Sequoia, Greylock, Benchmark, Founders Fund, Index, etc.) and respected angels saying is the next big opportunity in AI for 2026-2027? Summarize their theses with direct quotes and sources. Cite.

What new AI primitives are emerging in 2026 that could become large categories: verifiable inference, agent payments (x402/stablecoin), agent identity/auth, long-term memory, code sandboxes, simulation/world models, on-device agents, AI-native databases? State of each, who is building, and how big the opening is. Cite sources.
What AI markets and segments have strong demand and real budget but NO dominant winner yet in 2026 - wide-open categories? For each: the demand evidence and why it's still open. Cite sources.
What 'picks and shovels' for the AI and agent economy are underbuilt in 2026 - infrastructure that nearly every AI company needs but that is missing, immature, or fragmented? Cite sources.
State of physical AI / robotics, voice AI, and AI hardware startups in 2026 - funding, traction, leaders, and whitespace. Cite sources.

What new businesses and categories is AI regulation creating in 2026 (EU AI Act enforcement, US state laws, content provenance/disclosure, audit, model transparency)? Who is winning and what's still open? Cite sources.
What recent (2025-2026) AI research breakthroughs or techniques are not yet productized but could become startups or products? Cite specific papers and explain the commercial opening. Cite sources.

Map the AI eval / observability / testing landscape in 2026: top players (Braintrust, LangSmith, Langfuse, Galileo, Patronus, Arize, Comet/Opik), their funding/valuation, what each owns, pricing, and the gaps no one fills well. Cite sources.

Map the AI gateway / routing landscape in 2026 (Portkey, LiteLLM, OpenRouter, Cloudflare AI Gateway, Martian, Kong, TensorZero): players, funding/valuation, pricing, what's commoditized, and the gaps. Cite sources.

Map the distillation / fine-tuning-as-a-service and 'own your model' landscape in 2026 (OpenPipe, Predibase, distil labs, Together, Fireworks, Baseten, Modal): players, funding, what's commoditized, and the gaps. Cite sources.
Map the on-device / edge inference and small-model deployment landscape in 2026 (Ollama, llama.cpp ecosystem, quantization tooling, local agents): players, traction, and the gaps. Cite sources.

Map the AI provenance / verifiability / attestation landscape in 2026 (C2PA, signed receipts, zkML, TEE/confidential inference - Lagrange, Tinfoil, Phala, 0G, EQTY): players, funding, REAL paying demand vs hype, and the gaps. Cite sources.

Map the AI coding-tools landscape in 2026 (Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Cognition/Devin, Windsurf, Replit, Augment): revenue/traction, what's been commoditized, and remaining gaps. Cite sources.

Map the AI data infrastructure landscape in 2026 (vector DBs, context/RAG, data labeling, synthetic data, feature stores, unstructured ETL): players, funding, what's commoditized by long context and native platform features, and the gaps. Cite sources.

Map the voice-AI and conversational-AI agent landscape in 2026 (voice agents, speech models, telephony AI, ElevenLabs, Vapi, Retell, Cartesia, etc.): players, funding, traction, and the gaps. Cite sources.
